seoTitle: Edit a Confluence Page: 2026 Editor Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to edit a Confluence page in 2026, from entering edit mode and using slash commands to reviewing page history and publishing updates safely.
intro: Keeping documentation current means editing pages quickly without breaking formatting or losing track of changes. This guide covers how to open, modify, and publish updates to a Confluence page in 2026.

steps.heading: Enter edit mode
steps.body: Open your target page and click the pencil icon, or press the E key, to unlock the editing canvas.

steps.heading: Modify the content
steps.body: Move your cursor into the main content frame to add, remove, or reformat text and headings.

steps.heading: Use slash commands
steps.body: Type `/` to instantly drop in macros, callout boxes, and layout sections without leaving the keyboard.

steps.heading: Collaborate live
steps.body: Track teammates' live cursors and manage inline comments while co-editing.

steps.heading: Check page history
steps.body: Click the ellipsis menu and select Page History to audit past changes, compare revisions, and roll back if needed.

steps.heading: Publish your edits
steps.body: Toggle the Notify Watchers setting as needed, then click Publish to push your changes live.

faq.question: How do I quickly enter edit mode on a Confluence page?
faq.answer: Click the pencil icon at the top of the page, or use the keyboard shortcut E to jump straight into edit mode.

faq.question: Can multiple people edit a page at the same time?
faq.answer: Yes, Confluence's collaborative editing handles simultaneous typing from multiple users and helps prevent conflicting saves.

faq.question: Can I undo a page edit after publishing?
faq.answer: Yes, the page history log lets you compare revisions and roll back to an earlier version.

faq.question: Will teammates get notified every time I edit a page?
faq.answer: You control this with the Notify Watchers toggle before publishing, so you can choose whether to send an update notification.
